behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about the CLI’s externally visible stdout contract for real repository history, and rename detection depends on real git status/diff semantics across modules (repo read -> classification -> report). A unit-only parser test can miss failures in the real handoff from git output to categorization. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency (so not Agentic) and no multi-agentic chain (so not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report shows 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that change set.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior is often specific to real git/subprocess output and error handling. The behavior explicitly requires no crash plus correct externally visible counting in CLI output, which is a real contract check at the process boundary. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real git repo, add/modify a binary file, invoke the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success and stdout includes the expected binary file count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an external error-path contract for a real repo state that commonly triggers real git errors (e.g., unborn HEAD). Mocked/unit paths can hide subprocess exit/error-shape differences. This matches the System triad error-path trigger. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence; no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful/non-fatal behavior and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: The behavior requires round-trip validation against actual repository history and externally visible report output, not just internal counting logic. Real git history interpretation plus CLI output contract must be proven end-to-end. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert reported counts exactly match the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics rely on real git index/worktree behavior and how the CLI gathers data through real repository state; mocks can mask these differences. The behavior checks external stdout equivalence across two real states, which is an end-to-end contract check. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality and no multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, produce the same content change once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both cases, and assert structured stdout outputs are identical.
